goal: Reconstruct a blank Roblox place into a deterministic underwater whole-cell environment from package manifests.
changes: Set the main builder to GeneratedWater terrain mode for the blank-place reset.
Added deterministic Terrain water generation at center Vector3.new(0, 90, 0) with size Vector3.new(760, 240, 900).
Moved the spawn to Vector3.new(0, 90, -385.691) facing the cell center.
Removed default blank-place Baseplate and default SpawnLocation during rebuild.
Stopped depending on legacy raw environment packages for this reset and rebuilt from WholeCellOrganelleAssets.lua through WholeCellOrganelleMap.lua.
Preserved raw package loads under Workspace.MeshLibrary and assembled visible clones under Workspace.CellEnvironment_Main.ImportedOrganelles.
Scaled and spread the organelle map so the nucleus, ER, Golgi, mitochondria, vesicles, cytoskeleton, membrane, and cytoplasm read as one proportional underwater cell scene.
Kept gameplay minimal by using native Roblox water swimming and no custom buoyancy controller.
Preserved the nucleus entry portal on Workspace.Nucleus_Model with PortalControlledProxy collision.
Kept old outer-cell entry helpers removed: no CellEntryPoreGuide and no old cell wall gate.

representative_model_sizes.Nucleus_Model: visible mesh scale target 120; full bounding box includes portal/trail and measured approximately 120, 70.9, 194.4 studs

validation: Studio bridge rebuild and object/material probe succeeded.

visual_review.details: Computer Use get_app_state for RobloxStudio timed out with Apple event error -10005. macOS screencapture attempts captured Finder instead of the Studio viewport. Current evidence is from live Studio bridge probes, not a confirmed viewport screenshot.

next_recommended_phase: Run a manual Studio edit-mode and play-mode visual pass in the open place, then tune organelle positions only if the camera/player view shows a clear scale or placement problem.
